points.text: Assign a strictly increasing sequence number to each event per source; clients drop messages with lower numbers.
Include a server timestamp and sequence; if a message arrives out‑of‑order, buffer or discard based on policy.
Maintain a short sliding window of recent message IDs per client to detect and ignore duplicates.
Use short‑lived JWTs with a nonce claim; verify nonce freshness on each new WebSocket connection.
Rotate signing keys regularly and keep a revocation list to prevent replayed tokens.
Optionally sign each payload with HMAC using the session key; verify signature on receipt for integrity.
Implement exponential backoff on client reconnects to reduce connection storms and duplicate events.
Use monotonically increasing sequence numbers per event; client discards out-of-order or duplicate numbers.
Include server-generated event IDs (UUID) and store last seen IDs to dedupe.
On reconnect, client sends last received sequence number; server resumes from next.
Secure WebSocket with wss (TLS) and use short-lived signed JWTs; verify expiration.
Implement token replay protection by including a nonce or jti claim and tracking used tokens until they expire.
Enable TCP keepalive on both ends to detect dead connections quickly and trigger reconnection.
Optionally use application ACKs so server knows events were processed before advancing sequence.
Upgrade WebSocket to wss (TLS) to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and protection against man‑in‑the‑middle attacks.
Authenticate the upgrade request with a short‑lived signed JWT or session cookie and verify it on every new connection.
Include a per‑message monotonic sequence number or server‑issued nonce; server discards out‑of‑order or duplicate numbers.
Store the highest accepted sequence per client (or recent message IDs) in memory/DB and reject any replayed IDs.
Sign each payload with an HMAC using a connection‑specific secret derived from the auth token; verify on receipt.
Add a timestamp with a narrow acceptance window (e.g., ±30 s) and reject messages outside the window to limit replay.
Limit reconnection rate and enforce token rotation on each new WebSocket handshake to reduce token reuse risk.
Validate all incoming data against a strict schema and sanitize to prevent injection or XSS via downstream rendering.
